preferred_translation: one feels two feelings: bodily and mental
definition: SN 36.6's untrained painful-feeling line for the added mental feeling produced by reaction.
notes: Use this record for the two-feelings contrast in SN 36.6. Keep bodily and mental explicit so the second-arrow simile remains tied to reactive doubling rather than to pain in general.

example_phrases.translation: one feels two feelings: bodily and mental

authority_basis.scope: Supports the two-feelings line that names reactive doubling under painful feeling.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the untrained two-feelings contrast in SN 36.6.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten the line into generic suffering language or erase the bodily / mental distinction.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the second-arrow teaching from losing the practical difference between bodily pain and added mental reaction.
